paper.title: In utero delivery of targeted ionizable lipid nanoparticles facilitates in vivo gene editing of hematopoietic stem cells
paper.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

formulations.targeting_details: Anti-CD45 antibody conjugated to LNP surface. CD45 is expressed on all hematopoietic cells (broader than CD117). STEM LNP = Systematically optimized Targeted Editing Machinery.
formulations.formulation_notes: CD45-targeted LNP for in utero delivery. Different receptor than CD117 (used by Shi/Anderson and Breda). CD45 is pan-hematopoietic marker. Protein corona analysis: 94% of 859 adsorbed proteins were not differentially abundant between untargeted and CD45-targeted LNPs.

results.liver_metric: CD45-targeted vs untargeted comparison in hepatocytes (CD45-/CD31-) vs HSCs (LSK)
results.liver_notes: CD45-targeted LNPs increased HSC delivery relative to untargeted. Hepatocyte delivery was also present (fetal liver is the HSC niche at E13.5). Colocalization of CD45 and RFP confirmed delivery to CD45+ cells.

safety.tolerability_notes: Safe in utero delivery demonstrated. No adverse effects on fetal development noted.

experiments.notes: In utero IV delivery at E13.5. HSCs reside in fetal liver at this stage, making them MORE accessible than adult BM HSCs. CD45 targeting improved delivery to HSCs vs untargeted. Durable gene editing demonstrated.
Proof-of-concept CRISPR editing in fetal HSCs after single in utero injection. Quantitative editing % not extracted from abstract. Full paper needed for numbers.

key_findings_for_project: CD45 receptor targeting (alternative to CD117) — broader hematopoietic coverage
In utero delivery exploits fetal liver HSC niche — avoids BM access barrier entirely
STEM LNP platform from Mitchell/Weissman labs (UPenn) — known ionizable lipid library
Protein corona minimally altered by CD45 antibody conjugation (94% of proteins unchanged)
Safe and durable gene editing in fetal HSCs demonstrated
TAG AS exclude_from_training: in utero delivery context is incomparable to adult IV
Relevance: demonstrates CD45 as alternative targeting receptor for HSCs

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Different receptor (CD45 vs CD117). Different delivery context (in utero vs adult IV). Both use antibody-targeted LNPs.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Both use antibody targeting but different receptors (CD45 vs CD117) and contexts (fetal vs adult).
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Completely different approach (targeted vs untargeted, fetal vs adult).
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: Cannot place on adult Pareto — different delivery context. Should be annotated as 'exclude_from_training' but included in comparison.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Opens a new therapeutic modality: in utero gene correction before disease onset. Relevant for thalassemia major (hydrops fetalis).
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Mitchell/Weissman lab ionizable lipids may be in external databases. CD45 targeting is a new receptor variable. But in utero context makes direct comparison impossible.

limitations_and_caveats: In utero delivery is a fundamentally different context than adult IV — fetal HSCs are in liver, not BM
Tag as exclude_from_training for ML model — incomparable delivery biology
Quantitative editing % not extracted from abstract
CD45 is pan-hematopoietic — targets all blood cells, not just HSCs (less specific than CD117)
Translation to human in utero delivery faces enormous regulatory barriers
